statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, find the subarray with the maximum sum and print its elements. A subarray is a contiguous part of an array. For example, given the array [-2, 1, -3, 4, -1, 2, 1, -5, 4], the subarray with the maximum sum is [4, -1, 2, 1] with a sum of 6. If there are multiple subarrays with the same maximum sum, print any one of them.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer N, the number of elements in the array. The second line contains N integers, the elements of the array.
statement.outputFormat: The first line contains a single integer, the maximum sum of a subarray. The second line contains the elements of the subarray with the maximum sum, separated by spaces.

constraints.description: The number of elements in the array

examples.explanation: The subarray [4, -1, 2, 1] has the maximum sum of 6.
The subarray [-1] has the maximum sum of -1.

hints.text: Start by initializing two variables: max_sum to store the maximum sum found so far, and current_sum to store the sum of the current subarray. Initialize max_sum to the smallest possible integer value and current_sum to 0.

Iterate through the array, updating current_sum to be the sum of the current element and current_sum. If current_sum becomes negative, reset it to 0. Update max_sum if current_sum is greater than max_sum.

When the iteration is complete, the maximum sum subarray must be a contiguous subarray ending at the last element of the array. Find the maximum sum subarray ending at the last element by iterating backwards through the array and updating current_sum to be the sum of the current element and current_sum. Update max_sum if current_sum is greater than max_sum.

The maximum sum subarray must be a contiguous subarray ending at the last element of the array and starting at some point in the past. To find this, iterate backwards through the array and update start to be the index of the first element of the maximum sum subarray ending at the current index.
The maximum sum subarray is the subarray from start to the current index. Print the elements of this subarray.

editorial.title: Max Subarray Problem
editorial.intuition: The problem requires finding a subarray with the maximum sum within a given array of integers. Initially, it seems that checking every possible subarray would be the most straightforward approach, which is indeed correct but results in a high time complexity.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force solution involves iterating over the array and checking every possible subarray by summing its elements and comparing it with the current maximum sum. This approach, although correct, has a time complexity of O(n^2) due to the nested loops, making it inefficient for large inputs.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient solution can be achieved by using Kadane's algorithm, which iterates over the array only once and keeps track of the maximum sum of a subarray ending at each position.
editorial.optimalSolution: Kadane's algorithm works by initializing the maximum sum and the current sum to the first element of the array. Then, it iterates over the rest of the array, updating the current sum by adding the current element. If the current sum becomes negative, it is reset to the current element. The maximum sum is updated whenever the current sum exceeds it. This approach ensures that the maximum sum of a subarray is found in linear time.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of Kadane's algorithm can be proven by showing that it considers all possible subarrays and keeps track of the maximum sum seen so far. The key insight is that a subarray with a negative sum has no contribution to the overall maximum sum, so it can be safely discarded.
editorial.dryRun: To test the solution, a dry run can be performed with a sample input, such as an array with both positive and negative numbers, to verify that the algorithm correctly identifies the subarray with the maximum sum.
commonMistakes: Not handling the case where the maximum sum is negative
Not resetting the current sum when it becomes negative
Not updating the maximum sum correctly
Not considering the case where the input array is empty
Not handling the case where the input array contains only one element
